chief_complaint: A 35-year-old female patient with type 1 diabetes mellitus presented with abdominal pain and vomiting.
history: The patient developed diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) that was exacerbated by acute pancreatitis and severe hypertriglyceridemia. She had a history of type 1 diabetes mellitus and was notable for xanthomas. Laboratory tests showed extremely high triglyceride levels and milky plasma. The patient received IV insulin treatment and lipid-lowering medications.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination notable for xanthomas.
labs: Laboratory tests: extremely high triglyceride levels, milky plasma, biochemical evidence of DKA.
